failure_modes.text: Base-rate neglect lets vivid case details displace the general frequency of the outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: Ignoring the general frequency of an outcome because specific case details are compelling, vivid, or emotionally salient.
failure_modes.note: Compressed the table row into a chunk-safe failure statement.
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor to the outside view first: collect historical frequency data for the reference class before incorporating case-specific details.

failure_modes.text: Availability or substitution bias can push the team into the wrong reference class.
failure_modes.source_quote: Substituting a simpler, more readily available model ... often resulting in selecting the wrong reference class.
failure_modes.note: Preserves the failure mode while shortening the table wording.
failure_modes.mitigation: Scrutinize reference class fit: line up all assumptions that underpin the chosen class and test whether the current case matches each one.

failure_modes.text: Inside-view overconfidence can overwhelm the outside view and distort the prior.
failure_modes.source_quote: Over-reliance on personal experience and beliefs, often amplified in smart people who spin better narratives for their own viewpoint.
failure_modes.note: Turns the raw description into a chunk that fits later challenge lanes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Cultivate rationality and humility: recognize that high intelligence does not confer immunity to bias; adopt radical open-mindedness about the base-rate data.

premortem_questions.text: What is the average success/failure rate for projects or situations like this, regardless of our unique details?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the average success/failure rate for projects/situations like this, regardless of our unique details?

premortem_questions.text: Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Do the underlying structures and core assumptions of this reference group actually match our current situation, or are we confusing familiarity with fit?

premortem_questions.text: If a believable expert saw only the statistics and not our story, what outcome would they call most likely?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If a believable expert, viewing only the statistics and not my personal narrative, were to assess this, what would they say is the most likely outcome?
premortem_questions.note: Shortened the question slightly for chunk readability.

heuristics.text: Anchor to the outside view before arguing from the particulars of the case.
heuristics.source_quote: Anchoring to the Outside View: The primary actionable framework derived from this model is the discipline of actively anchoring first to the outside view.
heuristics.note: Retains the named heuristic while tightening the action.

heuristics.text: Use reference class forecasting to compare the case against structurally similar outcomes.
heuristics.source_quote: Reference Class Forecasting: This involves defining a group of past projects or outcomes that are structurally similar to the current situation.
heuristics.note: Directly grounded in the named playbook heuristic.

heuristics.text: Apply the Bayes or conditional-probability approach by starting with the prior and then updating with the new evidence.
heuristics.source_quote: The Bayes/Conditional Probability Approach ... incorporating the prior probability (the base rate) before integrating the new, specific evidence.
heuristics.note: Keeps the raw Bayesian sequence while making it chunk-ready.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on explicit outside-view intervention content and kept all items raw-backed rather than donor-shaped.
donor_drops: Dropped donor case-example select_when language because this layer is for intervention content, not routing repeats.
open_questions: A later relation-focused wave could preserve the ally links to System 2 and scientific method more structurally.

allies: Raw allies section is rich on Outside View, System 2 Thinking, Analogy and Reference Classes, and the Scientific Method.
antagonists: Raw antagonists section gives useful future relation material around System 1, motivated reasoning, and cognitive entrenchment.
structured_tensions: Structured tensions explicitly connect base-rates to representativeness-heuristic and confirmation-bias.
